title: VAN CLEEF & ARPELS: THE ALHAMBRA AT FIFTY-FIVE
description: A four-leaf clover motif that has adorned royalty, rock stars, and rebels alike — the Alhambra remains one of jewellery's greatest democratic luxuries.
content:
In 1968, Van Cleef & Arpels introduced a necklace unlike anything the Place Vendôme had seen before. The Alhambra — named for the Moorish palace in Granada whose latticed stone screens inspired its quatrefoil motif — was immediately democratic in a way that luxury jewellery rarely allowed itself to be.

The four-leaf clover shape was familiar, approachable, even folkloric. The materials — golden bezels, stones that ranged from onyx to turquoise to malachite — were rich without being austere. The price, relative to the most rarefied pieces on the square, was accessible to a much wider audience than the grandes maisons typically addressed.

Fifty-five years later, the Alhambra remains in continuous production. It has inspired counterfeits on every continent and appeared in the collections of women whose tastes span every decade and disposition: Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis and Rihanna, Queen Maxima of the Netherlands and various Silicon Valley figures who wear it as their sole concession to ornament.

For its anniversary, Van Cleef & Arpels has revisited the archive to produce a series of limited editions that work with materials the original design never contemplated. A piece set with fragments of meteorite, its surface dimpled by the same cosmic forces that shaped the solar system. Another in coral, sourced responsibly and cut to preserve the material's natural variation. A third — the most surprising — in carved bamboo, its surface lacquered in an amber that glows like honey caught in light.

The Alhambra at fifty-five is proof that some ideas are simply correct. Not fashionable, not trending — correct. The motif works because it is rooted in something older than the house itself, and broader than any single culture: the human desire to find luck in the shape of a leaf.
keywords: Van Cleef & Arpels, Alhambra, fine jewellery, luxury, Paris, clover, anniversary
